name: service-mesh-observability
description: Implement comprehensive observability for service meshes including distributed tracing, metrics, and visualization. Use when setting up mesh monitoring, debugging latency issues, or implementing SLOs for service communication.
capabilities: Provides Prometheus scrape and ServiceMonitor templates for Istio mesh metrics.
Supplies PromQL queries for request rate, error rate, and latency by service.
Includes Jaeger deployment and Istio tracing configuration with sampling settings.
Lists Linkerd viz install commands for dashboard and traffic inspection.
Offers Kiali configuration for mesh topology visualization.
Defines OpenTelemetry Collector and Istio Telemetry v2 settings.
author: wshobson
securitySummary: Pure documentation skill containing YAML templates, PromQL queries, and CLI examples for service mesh observability. All static findings are false positives: the scanner misinterpreted PromQL metric names (containing 'md5', 'sha' substrings) as weak crypto, flagged documentation links as network IOCs, and misidentified YAML field names as path traversal. The content is static documentation that matches its stated purpose exactly.
valueStatement: Service mesh telemetry is hard to wire across traces, metrics, and dashboards. This skill provides ready templates and queries for Istio and Linkerd observability.
userTitle: Set up service mesh observability fast
limitations: Does not deploy resources or run commands automatically.
Covers Istio and Linkerd examples only.
Does not validate your cluster configuration or alert thresholds.

useCases.title: Stand up mesh monitoring
useCases.description: Use templates to wire Prometheus, Grafana, and tracing for a new service mesh.

useCases.title: Investigate latency spikes
useCases.description: Apply PromQL queries and tracing setup to locate high latency services.

useCases.title: Define mesh SLOs
useCases.description: Use golden signal guidance to frame SLOs and alert rules for services.

promptTemplates.title: Quick start
promptTemplates.prompt: List the minimal steps and templates to enable Istio metrics and tracing in a new cluster.

promptTemplates.title: Dashboards
promptTemplates.prompt: Provide the key PromQL queries for request rate, error rate, and P99 latency by service.

promptTemplates.title: Tracing rollout
promptTemplates.prompt: Give an IstioOperator and Jaeger deployment example for distributed tracing.

promptTemplates.title: Full stack
promptTemplates.prompt: Combine Prometheus, Grafana, Jaeger, Kiali, and OTel templates into a staged rollout plan.

bestPractices: Sample tracing at high rates in dev and lower in production to control costs.
Use consistent trace context propagation across all services.
Alert on golden signals with clear thresholds defined in PrometheusRule.
antiPatterns: Collecting high cardinality labels without limits on Prometheus.
Running 100 percent tracing in production by default.
Operating without dashboards for service dependencies and topology.

faq.question: Is this compatible with Istio and Linkerd?
faq.answer: Yes, it provides examples for both Istio and Linkerd observability workflows.

faq.question: What are the limits of this skill?
faq.answer: It provides templates and guidance only. It does not deploy or validate configurations.

faq.question: Can I integrate with OpenTelemetry?
faq.answer: Yes, it includes an OpenTelemetry Collector config and Istio Telemetry example.

faq.question: Does it access my data or credentials?
faq.answer: No, it contains static documentation and does not access files or environment data.

faq.question: What if my metrics are missing?
faq.answer: Verify Prometheus scrape targets, service labels, and Istio telemetry settings are correct.

faq.question: How does it compare to vendor tools?
faq.answer: It is vendor neutral and uses common open source components and queries.
